summary: In-vehicle diagnostic file-transfer service aborts (SIGABRT) while building a temporary upload filename under memory pressure
description: On the IVI head unit the diagnostic log file-transfer helper (dlt-system) dies with SIGABRT while preparing a queued log/coredump file for upload. The abort fires in unique_name() as it allocates the buffer for the freshly minted temporary filename: when the unit is under sustained memory pressure (a large transfer backlog is drained right after a daemon reconnect), the allocation returns NULL and the code takes a hard abort() path (the MALLOC_ASSERT 'Out of memory' guard) instead of skipping the file, tearing down the whole file-transfer worker thread. The native arm64 backtrace bottoms out at abort() called directly from unique_name in /usr/bin/dlt-system, reached via send_one -> flush_dir_original while draining the compress/send directories. Expected: a transient allocation failure for a single file should be logged and that file skipped/retried, not abort the service. Impact: on-board file and coredump upload stops until the service is restarted, and it recurs whenever memory is tight.

journal excerpt (preceding the crash):
  [ 4471.882] INFO     file-transfer worker: reconnected to daemon, draining queued upload backlog
  [ 4471.884] WARN     dlt-system-filetransfer.c: heap allocation for temporary filename returned NULL (len=255)
  [ 4471.884] ERROR    unique_name: cannot allocate temporary upload filename buffer under memory pressure
  [ 4471.885] FATAL    ft-worker aborting (SIGABRT) from unique_name: 'Out of memory' while preparing a queued file for send
